opening_hook.title: The Station Nightclub Fire
opening_hook.text: February 20, 2003. A fire started by pyrotechnics during a Great White concert at The Station nightclub in West Warwick, Rhode Island. While the fire itself was deadly, the primary cause of the 100 fatalities was inadequate means of egress. Panicked patrons rushed toward the main entrance—the way they came in—ignoring other exits. Many exits were poorly marked, some were locked, and the exit doors opened inward, trapping people against them as the crowd pushed forward.
key_lessons: All exits must be clearly marked and visible
Exit doors must open in the direction of egress
Patrons must be informed about available exits
Multiple exit options are essential for crowd safety

core_concept.title: Means of Egress: The Life Safety Path
core_concept.definition: A continuous and unobstructed path of vertical and horizontal egress travel from any occupied portion of a building or structure to a public way.

three_components.description: The portion of the egress system that leads from any occupied portion of a building or structure to an exit
The portion of the egress system separated from other interior spaces by fire-resistance-rated construction
The portion of the egress system between the termination of an exit and a public way

content.title: Comprehensive Venue Assessment Tool
content.description: Conduct a systematic assessment of venue safety features using this interactive evaluation form.

checklist_items.item: Number of exits adequate for occupant load
checklist_items.verification: Count exits and verify against code requirements
checklist_items.pass_criteria: Minimum 2 exits remote from each other

checklist_items.item: Exit doors swing in direction of egress
checklist_items.verification: Physically check each exit door
checklist_items.failure_consequence: Crowd crush hazard—doors cannot open against pressure

checklist_items.item: Exit pathways clear of obstructions
checklist_items.verification: Walk each egress path completely

checklist_items.item: Exit signs visible and illuminated
checklist_items.verification: Check visibility from all areas of venue

checklist_items.item: Panic hardware installed and functional
checklist_items.verification: Test each panic bar or push pad
checklist_items.requirement: Single motion release, no keys or tools needed

checklist_items.item: Emergency lighting operational
checklist_items.verification: Test with power interruption simulation

checklist_items.item: Fire extinguishers properly located and accessible
checklist_items.verification: Check placement and travel distances

checklist_items.item: Correct extinguisher types for hazards present
checklist_items.verification: Match extinguisher types to fire classes in each area

checklist_items.item: Extinguishers inspected and charged
checklist_items.verification: Check inspection tags and pressure gauges

checklist_items.item: Fire alarm system operational
checklist_items.verification: Test alarms and verify monitoring connection

checklist_items.item: Sprinkler systems unobstructed
checklist_items.verification: Check 18in clearance below sprinklers

checklist_items.item: Electrical panels accessible and labeled
checklist_items.verification: Verify 36in clearance in front of panels

checklist_items.item: Temporary wiring properly installed
checklist_items.verification: Check for licensed electrician certification

checklist_items.item: GFCI protection where required
checklist_items.verification: Test outdoor and wet location circuits

checklist_items.item: Cables protected from damage and trip hazards
checklist_items.verification: Check cable ramps, overhead runs, secure taping

checklist_items.item: Stage and structures engineered and permitted
checklist_items.verification: Review stamped drawings and permits

checklist_items.item: Temporary structures meet NFPA 701 flame propagation
checklist_items.verification: Check certification for tents and fabrics

checklist_items.item: Barriers and guardrails secure and adequate
checklist_items.verification: Test stability and check heights

checklist_items.item: Overhead rigging properly installed
checklist_items.verification: Verify certified rigger involvement

checklist_items.item: Queue and crowd flow systems adequate
checklist_items.verification: Walk entry and exit flows at peak capacity

checklist_items.item: Viewing areas and seating properly configured
checklist_items.verification: Check sight lines and density calculations

checklist_items.item: Steward and security positions strategically placed
checklist_items.verification: Verify visibility and communication coverage

CRITICAL.description: Critical safety issue, event cannot proceed

content.title: Virtual Fire Safety Inspection
content.description: Walk through a virtual venue as a fire safety inspector and identify code violations before they become hazards.

inspector_scenario.title: Pre-Event Fire Inspection
inspector_scenario.context: You're conducting a final fire safety inspection 24 hours before a 3,000-person indoor concert. The fire marshal will arrive in 2 hours. Identify and correct any violations.

violations.code_reference: NFPA 101: Means of egress must remain unobstructed
violations.correction: Remove equipment immediately, establish clear 36in path

violations.description: Fire extinguisher behind stack of chairs
violations.code_reference: NFPA 10: Extinguishers must be readily accessible
violations.correction: Clear access to extinguisher, mark location clearly

violations.description: Exit sign above rear door not illuminated
violations.code_reference: Exit signs must be illuminated when building is occupied
violations.correction: Replace bulb or battery, test emergency power

violations.description: Overloaded power strip with multiple high-draw devices
violations.code_reference: NEC: Power strips cannot be daisy-chained or overloaded
violations.correction: Redistribute load to multiple circuits, use proper power distro

violations.description: Flammable materials stored near electrical panels
violations.code_reference: NFPA 1: 36in clearance required in front of panels
violations.correction: Relocate materials, establish and mark clearance zone

violations.description: Exit door equipped with keyed lock instead of panic hardware
violations.code_reference: Panic hardware required on exit doors in assembly occupancies

classes.fuels: Ordinary combustibles: paper, wood, fabrics, rubber
classes.extinguishers: Water, foam, dry chemical, clean agent

classes.fuels: Flammable liquids and gases: gasoline, oil, grease, propane
classes.extinguishers: Dry chemical, CO2, foam (NOT water stream)

extinguisher_selector_tool.title: Fire Extinguisher Selection Tool
extinguisher_selector_tool.description: Select the appropriate extinguisher type for each hazard scenario

scenarios.location: Merchandise area with paper products and cardboard boxes
scenarios.incorrect_selection_warning: CO2 and Class B agents may not be effective on deep-seated Class A fires

scenarios.location: Generator area with diesel fuel present
scenarios.incorrect_selection_warning: Water will spread flammable liquid fires!

scenarios.location: Audio equipment rack with energized amplifiers
scenarios.incorrect_selection_warning: Water and foam conduct electricity—electrocution hazard!

scoring.time_bonus: Up to 10 points for completing under time limit

content.title: Fire Emergency Response Planning
content.description: Develop fire safety plans and respond to fire emergency scenarios.

fire_response_scenario.title: Fire Emergency Response
fire_response_scenario.scenario: During the event, smoke is reported in the backstage area near the electrical distribution. A small fire is visible near an overloaded power strip.

decision_tree.prompt: You receive a radio call: 'Smoke backstage near distro, possible fire.' What is your FIRST action?

options.feedback: A full evacuation may be premature. First confirm the fire and attempt to extinguish if safe. Premature evacuation can cause panic and injuries.

options.text: Go to the location, assess the fire, and call for appropriate extinguisher
options.feedback: Correct! First assess the situation to determine appropriate response. A small fire can often be safely extinguished.

options.text: Call 911 immediately and wait for fire department
options.feedback: While calling 911 is important, immediate action may prevent a small fire from becoming a major incident. Assess and act if safe to do so.

fire_extinguisher_use.title: Using a Fire Extinguisher: PASS Method

steps.action: Pull the pin to unlock the operating handle
Aim low, pointing the nozzle at the base of the fire
Squeeze the handle to release the extinguishing agent
Sweep from side to side at the base until the fire appears out

important_notes: Ensure you have a clear escape route before fighting the fire
If the fire doesn't immediately respond to the extinguisher, evacuate immediately
Never turn your back on a fire, even if it appears extinguished
Ensure the fire department is called even if you extinguish the fire

fire_watch_procedures.title: Fire Watch Requirements
when_required: Fire protection systems out of service
Events with pyrotechnics or open flames
duties: Documentation of all rounds and observations

questions.question: What is the maximum travel distance to a portable fire extinguisher?
questions.rationale: Section 4.6.8 states that generally, the maximum travel distance to an extinguisher should be 50 feet (15.24 m).

questions.question: What does an extinguisher rating of '1-A:10-B:C' mean?
options: 1 pound of chemical for class A, 10 pounds for class B and C
1.25 gallons water equivalent on Class A, 10 sq ft Class B capability
1 minute discharge time for Class A, 10 minutes for B and C
1 meter range for Class A, 10 meter range for B and C
questions.rationale: Section 4.6.5 explains: number before A × 1.25 = gallons of water equivalent. Number before B = square feet of Class B fire capability.

questions.question: What is the minimum stairway width required for means of egress?
questions.rationale: Section 4.3.3.8 states stairways must have a width of not less than 36 inches (0.914 m).

questions.question: Fire doors protecting exits may be propped open during events if monitored by staff.
questions.rationale: Section 4.3.3.12 states fire doors protecting exits should not be propped or blocked open as they are specially constructed fire-resistant doors.
